message:
fix(remote): correct is_finished semantics for closed result channels

RemoteHandle::is_finished() previously returned true when the oneshot
result channel was closed, even if no terminal result had been buffered.
This was incorrect: the sender may have been dropped before the remote
task reached a terminal lifecycle state (Running → Completed/Failed/
Cancelled). Callers relying on is_finished() to mean "the remote work
is done and the result is available" would skip the close()/abort()
fencing needed to properly drain the remote task.

Now is_finished() only returns true when:
- The result has already been consumed (self.completed), OR
- A terminal result is buffered and ready (self.receiver.is_ready())

A closed-but-empty channel requires the caller to call close() or
abort() to fence and transition the handle to a terminal state.

Adds a targeted regression test that:
1. Spawns a remote, marks it Running
2. Closes the sender channel while runtime task is still live
3. Asserts is_finished() is false (the fix)
4. Calls close() to properly transition to Cancelled
5. Asserts is_finished() is now true

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
